framework: Diátaxis (https://diataxis.fr) — 4 documentation types: Tutorial, How-to, Reference, Explanation

diataxis_types.tutorial: Learning-oriented. Guides a newcomer through a complete working example from zero. Goal: the reader succeeds at a concrete task.
diataxis_types.how_to: Task-oriented. Step-by-step guide for a specific goal the reader already has. Goal: accomplish X.
diataxis_types.reference: Information-oriented. Authoritative description of the system. Goal: accurate, complete lookup.
diataxis_types.explanation: Understanding-oriented. Discusses why decisions were made, trade-offs, background. Goal: insight.

strengths: Clear problem statement: 'AI-driven testing tools currently rely on screenshots + visual guessing — slow, fragile, and shallow'
Concise 6-primitive table explains the solution space at a glance
Code examples show the most important usage patterns (query, waitForPageReady, actAndWait, runAcrossDevices)
7 testing pain points table creates immediate resonance with target audience
Linkage model (6 path types) is well-explained with a table
Platform coverage table gives quick status overview
weaknesses: README code examples use 'page.probe.query()' shorthand that does not match the actual Playwright integration API (ProbePage wrapper). The real usage requires 'const probe = new ProbePage(page); const el = await probe.query("order-list")'.
No tutorial: there is no 'get started in 5 minutes' section with actual runnable steps
No installation instructions: no npm install command shown
Platform coverage table uses vague 'Phase X' labels rather than 'available now' / 'in progress'
Element model description is a flat list of 15 attribute names with no examples of what each looks like in practice
Linkage model explanation has no data-probe-linkage HTML example showing the JSON format

diataxis_gaps.tutorial: MISSING — No getting-started tutorial. A new user cannot get from 'npm install' to a working test.
diataxis_gaps.how_to: MISSING — No task-specific guides (e.g. 'How to test a data table', 'How to verify a filter-driven reload', 'How to add annotations to an existing React app').
diataxis_gaps.reference: PARTIAL — Shows some API methods in code examples but is not a complete method reference. No parameter types, return types, or error conditions documented.
diataxis_gaps.explanation: GOOD — Strong explanation of why the framework exists, the two-layer architecture, and the design rationale.

recommendations: Add a 'Quick Start' section with actual runnable steps: npm install → injectProbe setup → first test
Fix code examples to use the actual ProbePage API, not the shorthand
Add a 'How to annotate a form' and 'How to verify a filter+table linkage' guide
Expand element model section with a minimal working ProbeElement JSON example

strengths: Strong problem statement with quantified pain points (efficiency, stability, depth)
Clean mapping of 7 pain areas to 6 primitives combinations — shows architectural reasoning
Design principles table is explicit and valuable: zero-business-logic coupling, light annotation, production-zero impact, cross-platform, LLM-friendly
Annotation cost model (table with page complexity vs time) is a compelling adoption argument
Platform matrix with annotation + injection mechanisms is useful reference
Measurable success metrics table (accuracy, timing comparisons)
weaknesses: Written entirely in Chinese — not accessible to English-speaking contributors and users without translation
Element model shown as condensed TypeScript (one-liners) rather than the full annotated definition in spec/probe-types.ts
No code examples — only type signatures and architecture descriptions
Linkage path section is brief; no examples of what each path type looks like in an annotation
Phase roadmap is aspirational but not linked to current implementation state

diataxis_gaps.reference: PARTIAL — Phase descriptions and model structure provide reference value but are incomplete
diataxis_gaps.explanation: STRONG — The problem statement, design principles, and architecture reasoning are well-articulated

recommendations: Translate to English (or add an English version) for international accessibility
Link to spec/probe-types.ts as the authoritative source for type definitions rather than re-stating them inline
Add one concrete annotation example per platform in the platform coverage section

strengths: Most complete document in the project — covers ProbeElement model, ProbeType enum, Platform Context, all 6 linkage path types, full UITestProbe API, cross-platform SDK architecture, annotation cost model, project structure, and implementation roadmap
ProbeElement TypeScript interface in section 1 is fully annotated with Chinese comments explaining each field's purpose
Linkage model (section 2) includes concrete examples for each of the 6 path types with test scenario descriptions
Pre-check table in section 3 is well-formatted with condition, error code, and rejection response
Cross-platform annotation table in section 4 shows all 8 platforms with idiom examples
Phase 1 success criteria code block provides a concrete 'done' definition
actAndWait flow diagram in section 3 explains the action+wait+linkage pipeline clearly
weaknesses: Written entirely in Chinese prose — code examples are English but explanatory text is Chinese, making it inaccessible without translation
spec/conformance-tests/ is referenced in section 4 but does not exist in the repository — creates false expectation
Linkage examples use Chinese business domain terms (广东, province/city/district) that may confuse non-Chinese readers
Windows platform injection section (section 4) lacks implementation detail compared to web/Flutter/iOS
Project structure diagram (section 6) is slightly out of date — does not show windows-core/windows-wpf/windows-winform/windows-winui split
No versioning information or change history

diataxis_gaps.tutorial: MISSING — No walkthrough of implementing a new platform SDK or annotating a real application
diataxis_gaps.how_to: PARTIAL — Phase success criteria in section 7 functions as a 'how to verify success' guide but is not organized as how-to guides
diataxis_gaps.reference: STRONG — The most complete reference document in the project. Covers all key types, all API methods, all platforms.
diataxis_gaps.explanation: STRONG — Architecture diagrams, design principles section, and linkage model rationale provide good explanatory depth.

recommendations: Translate to English or add an English companion document
Remove reference to spec/conformance-tests/ until it exists, or create a minimal version
Update project structure to reflect the windows-core + windows-wpf/winform/winui split
Add a 'Implementing a new platform SDK' section as a guide for contributors

tutorial.description: No tutorial exists anywhere in the documentation. A developer cannot follow a step-by-step guide from zero to a working test. This is the most important missing piece for adoption.

how_to.description: No task-specific how-to guides exist. Common tasks like 'annotate a form', 'test a filter+table interaction', 'set up Playwright integration' have no dedicated documentation.

reference.description: DESIGN.md provides substantial reference coverage of types, API, and platforms. However, it is in Chinese and slightly out of date. spec/probe-types.ts is the authoritative machine-readable reference.

explanation.description: All three documents contribute explanation content. The 'why probe over screenshots', the 6-primitive rationale, and the linkage model reasoning are well-explained.

language_accessibility.issue: SPEC.md and DESIGN.md are written in Chinese. This limits accessibility for non-Chinese-speaking contributors and users.
language_accessibility.recommendation: At minimum, translate or summarize DESIGN.md into English as the primary technical reference.

critical_missing_documents.title: Getting Started: Test a Web App in 15 Minutes
critical_missing_documents.content: Install → annotate 3 elements → write first probe test → run with Playwright. Fully runnable with examples/web-react/.

critical_missing_documents.title: How to Annotate a React/Vue/Angular Page
critical_missing_documents.content: Which ProbeType to pick for common widgets, minimal annotation examples, data-probe-linkage JSON format guide.

critical_missing_documents.title: How to Set Up Playwright Integration
critical_missing_documents.content: npm install, build IIFE bundle, configure injectProbe() in playwright.config.ts, wrap Page in ProbePage, register matchers.

critical_missing_documents.title: How to Implement a Platform SDK
critical_missing_documents.content: The 3-layer pattern, required interface methods, pre-check pattern, known platform adaptation challenges.

critical_missing_documents.title: Complete API Reference (English)
critical_missing_documents.content: All 44 methods with signatures, parameters, return types, error conditions, and platform availability matrix.

top_3_recommendations: Write a 15-minute getting started tutorial against examples/web-react/ — this single document would have the highest adoption impact.
Translate DESIGN.md to English or write an equivalent English technical reference — the Chinese-only detailed docs are the biggest barrier for the international open-source audience.
Fix the README code examples to match the actual Playwright ProbePage API — the current examples show page.probe.query() which requires setup steps that are not shown.
